properties.definition: Overrides any value set in the Instrument metadata

properties.name: DetectorConfig__device_name
properties.definition: Must match a device defined in the instrument.json

classes.name: Device
classes.definition: Generic device

classes.name: Device
classes.definition: A scientific instrument used in BBQS data collection. Specialized by modality.

properties.name: category
properties.definition: BBQS canonical device category (one of the 32).

classes.name: Device
classes.definition: Metadata about a specific instance of a data acquisition device, e.g., recording system, electrode, microscope. Link to a DeviceModel.model to represent information about the model of the device.

properties.name: Device__description
properties.definition: Description of the device as free-form text. If there is any software/firmware associated with the device, the names and versions of those can be added to NWBFile.was_generated_by.

properties.name: Device__manufacturer
properties.definition: DEPRECATED. The name of the manufacturer of the device, e.g., Imec, Plexon, Thorlabs. Instead of using this field, store the value in DeviceModel.manufacturer and link to that DeviceModel from this Device.

properties.name: Device__model_name
properties.definition: DEPRECATED. The model name of the device, e.g., Neuropixels 1.0, V-Probe, Bergamo III. Instead of using this field, create and add a new DeviceModel named the model name and link to that DeviceModel from this Device.

properties.name: Device__model_number
properties.definition: DEPRECATED. The model number (or part/product number) of the device, e.g., PRB_1_4_0480_1, PLX-VP-32-15SE(75)-(260-80)(460-10)-300-(1)CON/32m-V, BERGAMO. Instead of using this field, store the value in DeviceModel.model_number and link to that DeviceModel from this Device.

properties.name: Device__serial_number
properties.definition: The serial number of the device.

classes.name: DeviceConfig
classes.definition: Parent class for all configurations

properties.name: DeviceConfig__device_name
properties.definition: Must match a device defined in the instrument.json

classes.name: DeviceModel
classes.definition: Model properties of a data acquisition device, e.g., recording system, electrode, microscope. This should be extended for specific types of device models to include additional attributes specific to each type. The name of the DeviceModel should be the most common representation of the model name, e.g., Neuropixels 1.0, V-Probe, Bergamo III.

properties.name: DeviceModel__description
properties.definition: Description of the device model as free-form text.

properties.name: DeviceModel__manufacturer
properties.definition: The name of the manufacturer of the device model, e.g., Imec, Plexon, Thorlabs.

properties.name: DeviceModel__model_number
properties.definition: The model number (or part/product number) of the device, e.g., PRB_1_4_0480_1, PLX-VP-32-15SE(75)-(260-80)(460-10)-300-(1)CON/32m-V, BERGAMO.

classes.name: Df
classes.definition: Dark-field microscopy imaging data

classes.name: DfOverF
classes.definition: dF/F information about a region of interest (ROI). Storage hierarchy of dF/F should be the same as for segmentation (i.e., same names for ROIs and for image planes).

classes.name: DiagnosticProcedure
classes.definition: A medical procedure intended primarily for diagnostic, as opposed to therapeutic, purposes.

classes.name: Dic
classes.definition: Differential interference contrast microscopy imaging data

classes.name: Digikey
classes.definition: Model DigiKey

classes.name: DigitalMicromirrorDevice
classes.definition: Description of a Digital Micromirror Device (DMD)

properties.name: DigitalMicromirrorDevice__dmd_curtain
properties.definition: DMD Curtain (fraction of DMD scan period)

properties.name: DigitalMicromirrorDevice__line_shear_anchors
properties.definition: Line shear anchors at top and bottom of field of view (pixels)

properties.name: DigitalMicromirrorDevice__pixel_size
properties.definition: DMD Pixel size (fraction of line scan period)

properties.name: DigitalMicromirrorDevice__start_phase
properties.definition: DMD Start phase (fraction of line scan period)

classes.name: Disc
classes.definition: Description of a running disc (i.e. MindScope Disc)

properties.name: Disc__encoder_firmware
properties.definition: Firmware to read from decoder chip counts

classes.name: Disorder
classes.definition: Biolink, SNOMED, or other identifier for disorder studied

properties.name: Disorder__identifier
properties.definition: The identifier can be any url or a compact URI, preferably supported by identifiers.org.

classes.name: Dodotronic
classes.definition: Model Dodotronic

classes.name: Doric
classes.definition: Model Doric

classes.name: Driver12Volts
classes.definition: Model Driver12Volts

classes.name: Drug
classes.definition: A chemical or biologic substance, used as a medical therapy, that has a physiological effect on an organism. Here the term drug is used interchangeably with the term medicine although clinical knowledge makes a clear difference between them.

properties.name: activeIngredient
properties.definition: An active ingredient, typically chemical compounds and/or biologic substances.

properties.name: administrationRoute
properties.definition: A route by which this drug may be administered, e.g. 'oral'.

properties.name: alcoholWarning
properties.definition: Any precaution, guidance, contraindication, etc. related to consumption of alcohol while taking this drug.

properties.name: availableStrength
properties.definition: An available dosage strength for the drug.

properties.name: breastfeedingWarning
properties.definition: Any precaution, guidance, contraindication, etc. related to this drug's use by breastfeeding mothers.

properties.name: clincalPharmacology
properties.definition: Description of the absorption and elimination of drugs, including their concentration (pharmacokinetics, pK) and biological effects (pharmacodynamics, pD).

properties.name: clinicalPharmacology
properties.definition: Description of the absorption and elimination of drugs, including their concentration (pharmacokinetics, pK) and biological effects (pharmacodynamics, pD).

properties.name: dosageForm
properties.definition: A dosage form in which this drug/supplement is available, e.g. 'tablet', 'suspension', 'injection'.

properties.name: doseSchedule
properties.definition: A dosing schedule for the drug for a given population, either observed, recommended, or maximum dose based on the type used.

properties.name: drugClass
properties.definition: The class of drug this belongs to (e.g., statins).

properties.name: drugUnit
properties.definition: The unit in which the drug is measured, e.g. '5 mg tablet'.

properties.name: foodWarning
properties.definition: Any precaution, guidance, contraindication, etc. related to consumption of specific foods while taking this drug.

properties.name: includedInHealthInsurancePlan
properties.definition: The insurance plans that cover this drug.

properties.name: interactingDrug
properties.definition: Another drug that is known to interact with this drug in a way that impacts the effect of this drug or causes a risk to the patient. Note: disease interactions are typically captured as contraindications.

properties.name: isAvailableGenerically
properties.definition: True if the drug is available in a generic form (regardless of name).

properties.name: isProprietary
properties.definition: True if this item's name is a proprietary/brand name (vs. generic name).

properties.name: labelDetails
properties.definition: Link to the drug's label details.

properties.name: legalStatus
properties.definition: The drug or supplement's legal status, including any controlled substance schedules that apply.

properties.name: maximumIntake
properties.definition: Recommended intake of this supplement for a given population as defined by a specific recommending authority.

properties.name: mechanismOfAction
properties.definition: The specific biochemical interaction through which this drug or supplement produces its pharmacological effect.

properties.name: nonProprietaryName
properties.definition: The generic name of this drug or supplement.

properties.name: overdosage
properties.definition: Any information related to overdose on a drug, including signs or symptoms, treatments, contact information for emergency response.

properties.name: pregnancyWarning
properties.definition: Any precaution, guidance, contraindication, etc. related to this drug's use during pregnancy.

properties.name: prescribingInfo
properties.definition: Link to prescribing information for the drug.

properties.name: prescriptionStatus
properties.definition: Indicates the status of drug prescription, e.g. local catalogs classifications or whether the drug is available by prescription or over-the-counter, etc.

properties.name: proprietaryName
properties.definition: Proprietary name given to the diet plan, typically by its originator or creator.

properties.name: relatedDrug
properties.definition: Any other drug related to this one, for example commonly-prescribed alternatives.

properties.name: rxcui
properties.definition: The RxCUI drug identifier from RXNORM.

properties.name: warning
properties.definition: Any FDA or other warnings about the drug (text or URL).

classes.name: Dseg
classes.definition:
A discrete segmentation.

This suffix may only be used in derivative datasets.

classes.name: Dwi
classes.definition: Diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI).

classes.name: Ealing
classes.definition: Model Ealing

classes.name: Ecephys
classes.definition: Model ecephys

classes.name: Edmund_Optics
classes.definition: Model Edmund Optics

classes.name: EducationEvent
classes.definition: Event type: Education event.

properties.name: assesses
properties.definition: The item being described is intended to assess the competency or learning outcome defined by the referenced term.

properties.name: educationalLevel
properties.definition: The level in terms of progression through an educational or training context. Examples of educational levels include 'beginner', 'intermediate' or 'advanced', and formal sets of level indicators.

properties.name: teaches
properties.definition: The item being described is intended to help a person learn the competency or learning outcome defined by the referenced term.

classes.name: Eeg
classes.definition: Electroencephalography

classes.name: ElectricalSeries
classes.definition: A time series of acquired voltage data from extracellular recordings. The data field is an int or float array storing data in volts. The first dimension should always represent time. The second dimension, if present, should represent channels.

properties.name: ElectricalSeries__channel_conversion
properties.definition: Channel-specific conversion factor. Multiply the data in the 'data' dataset by these values along the channel axis (as indicated by axis attribute) AND by the global conversion factor in the 'conversion' attribute of 'data' to get the data values in Volts, i.e., data in Volts = data * data.conversion * channel_conversion. This approach allows for both global and per-channel data conversion factors needed to support the storage of electrical recordings as native values generated by data acquisition systems. If this dataset is not present, then there is no channel-specific conversion factor, i.e., it is 1 for all channels.

properties.name: ElectricalSeries__electrodes
properties.definition: DynamicTableRegion pointer to the electrodes that this time series was generated from.

properties.name: ElectricalSeries__filtering
properties.definition: Filtering applied to all channels of the data. For example, if this ElectricalSeries represents high-pass-filtered data (also known as AP Band), then this value could be "High-pass 4-pole Bessel filter at 500 Hz". If this ElectricalSeries represents low-pass-filtered LFP data and the type of filter is unknown, then this value could be "Low-pass filter at 300 Hz". If a non-standard filter type is used, provide as much detail about the filter properties as possible.

classes.name: ElectrodeGroup
classes.definition: A physical grouping of electrodes, e.g., a shank of an array. An electrode group is typically used to describe electrodes that are physically connected on a single device and are often (but not always) used together for analysis, such as for spike sorting. Note that this is descriptive metadata; electrodes from different groups can still be spike-sorted together if needed.

properties.name: ElectrodeGroup__device
properties.definition: Link to the device that was used to record from this electrode group.

properties.name: ElectrodeGroup__location
properties.definition: Location of electrode group. Specify the area, layer, comments on estimation of area/layer, etc. Use standard atlas names for anatomical regions when possible.

properties.name: ElectrodeGroup__position
properties.definition: Stereotaxic or common framework coordinates of the electrode group position.

classes.name: Electrodes
classes.definition: File that gives the location of (i)EEG or EMG electrodes.

classes.name: ElectrodesTable
classes.definition: A table of all electrodes (i.e., channels) used for recording. Introduced in NWB 2.8.0. Replaces the "electrodes" table (neurodata_type_inc DynamicTable, no neurodata_type_def) that is part of NWBFile.

properties.name: ElectrodesTable__filtering
properties.definition: Description of hardware filtering, including the filter name and frequency cutoffs.

properties.name: ElectrodesTable__group
properties.definition: Reference to the ElectrodeGroup this electrode is a part of.

properties.name: ElectrodesTable__group_name
properties.definition: Name of the ElectrodeGroup this electrode is a part of.

properties.name: ElectrodesTable__imp
properties.definition: Impedance of the channel, in ohms.

properties.name: ElectrodesTable__location
properties.definition: Location of the electrode (channel). Specify the area, layer, comments on estimation of area/layer, stereotaxic coordinates if in vivo, etc. Use standard atlas names for anatomical regions when possible.

properties.name: ElectrodesTable__reference
properties.definition: Description of the reference electrode and/or reference scheme used for this electrode, e.g., "stainless steel skull screw" or "online common average referencing".

properties.name: ElectrodesTable__rel_x
properties.definition: x coordinate in electrode group. Units should be specified in microns.

properties.name: ElectrodesTable__rel_y
properties.definition: y coordinate in electrode group. Units should be specified in microns.

properties.name: ElectrodesTable__rel_z
properties.definition: z coordinate in electrode group. Units should be specified in microns.

properties.name: ElectrodesTable__x
properties.definition: x coordinate of the channel location in the brain (+x is posterior). Units should be specified in microns.

properties.name: ElectrodesTable__y
properties.definition: y coordinate of the channel location in the brain (+y is inferior). Units should be specified in microns.

properties.name: ElectrodesTable__z
properties.definition: z coordinate of the channel location in the brain (+z is right). Units should be specified in microns.

classes.name: Em
classes.definition: Model EM

classes.name: Emg
classes.definition: Model EMG

classes.name: Emg
classes.definition: Measurements of muscular activity.

classes.name: Emory_University
classes.definition: Model Emory University

classes.name: Enclosure
classes.definition: Description of an enclosure

classes.name: EnvironmentalSensor
classes.definition: Devices sensing the environment or spatial context around a subject (LiDAR, mmWave, GPS, RFID, flow, ultrasonic mic).

properties.name: category
properties.definition: BBQS canonical device category (one of the 32).

classes.name: Environmentsensor
classes.definition: Model EnvironmentSensor

classes.name: EphysAssembly
classes.definition: Named assembly for combining a manipulator and extracellular ephys probes

properties.name: EphysAssembly__probes
properties.definition: Probes that are held by this module

classes.name: EphysAssemblyConfig
classes.definition: Group of configurations for an ephys assembly

properties.name: EphysAssemblyConfig__device_name
properties.definition: Must match a device defined in the instrument.json

properties.name: EphysAssemblyConfig__modules
properties.definition: Configurations for conveniently tracking manipulator modules, e.g. on the New Scale dome.

classes.name: EphysProbe
classes.definition: Probe used in an extracellular ephys experiment

classes.name: Epi
classes.definition:
The phase-encoding polarity (PEpolar) technique combines two or more Spin Echo
EPI scans with different phase encoding directions to estimate the underlying
inhomogeneity/deformation map.

classes.name: Equipment
classes.definition: Equipment

classes.name: Equus_Asinus
classes.definition: Model Equus asinus

classes.name: EthicsApproval
classes.definition: Information about ethics committee approval for project

properties.name: EthicsApproval__contactPoint
properties.definition: Information about the ethics approval committee.

properties.name: EthicsApproval__identifier
properties.definition: Approved Protocol identifier, often a number or alphanumeric string.

classes.name: Euresys
classes.definition: Model Euresys

classes.name: Event
classes.definition: An event happening at a certain time and location, such as a concert, lecture, or festival. Ticketing information may be added via the [[offers]] property. Repeated events may be structured as separate Event objects.

properties.name: about
properties.definition: The subject matter of an object.

properties.name: actor
properties.definition: An actor (individual or a group), e.g. in TV, radio, movie, video games etc., or in an event. Actors can be associated with individual items or with a series, episode, clip.

properties.name: aggregateRating
properties.definition: The overall rating, based on a collection of reviews or ratings, of the item.

properties.name: attendee
properties.definition: A person or organization attending the event.

properties.name: audience
properties.definition: An intended audience, i.e. a group for whom something was created.

properties.name: composer
properties.definition: The person or organization who wrote a composition, or who is the composer of a work performed at some event.

properties.name: contributor
properties.definition: A secondary contributor to the CreativeWork or Event.

properties.name: director
properties.definition: A director of e.g. TV, radio, movie, video gaming etc. content, or of an event. Directors can be associated with individual items or with a series, episode, clip.

properties.name: duration
properties.definition: The duration of the item (movie, audio recording, event, etc.) in [ISO 8601 duration format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601).

properties.name: endDate
properties.definition: The end date and time of the item (in [ISO 8601 date format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)).

properties.name: eventAttendanceMode
properties.definition: The eventAttendanceMode of an event indicates whether it occurs online, offline, or a mix.

properties.name: eventSchedule
properties.definition:
Associates an [[Event]] with a [[Schedule]]. There are circumstances where it is preferable to share a schedule for a series of
      repeating events rather than data on the individual events themselves. For example, a website or application might prefer to publish a schedule for a weekly
      gym class rather than provide data on every event. A schedule could be processed by applications to add forthcoming events to a calendar. An [[Event]] that
      is associated with a [[Schedule]] using this property should not have [[startDate]] or [[endDate]] properties. These are instead defined within the associated
      [[Schedule]], this avoids any ambiguity for clients using the data. The property might have repeated values to specify different schedules, e.g. for different months
      or seasons.

properties.name: eventStatus
properties.definition: An eventStatus of an event represents its status; particularly useful when an event is cancelled or rescheduled.

properties.name: funder
properties.definition: A person or organization that supports (sponsors) something through some kind of financial contribution.

properties.name: funding
properties.definition: A [[Grant]] that directly or indirectly provide funding or sponsorship for this item. See also [[ownershipFundingInfo]].

properties.name: hasParticipationOffer
properties.definition: An offer to participate in the event, for example, Call for Proposals, Call for Speakers, or Call for Performers.

properties.name: hasSponsorshipOffer
properties.definition: An offer to sponsor the event, for example, Sponsorship Prospectus, Sponsorship Opportunities, or Sponsor Packages.

properties.name: inLanguage
properties.definition: The language of the content or performance or used in an action. Please use one of the language codes from the [IETF BCP 47 standard](http://tools.ietf.org/html/bcp47). See also [[availableLanguage]].

properties.name: isAccessibleForFree
properties.definition: A flag to signal that the item, event, or place is accessible for free.

properties.name: keywords
properties.definition: Keywords or tags used to describe some item. Multiple textual entries in a keywords list are typically delimited by commas, or by repeating the property.

properties.name: location
properties.definition: The location of, for example, where an event is happening, where an organization is located, or where an action takes place.

properties.name: maximumAttendeeCapacity
properties.definition: The total number of individuals that may attend an event or venue.

properties.name: maximumPhysicalAttendeeCapacity
properties.definition: The maximum physical attendee capacity of an [[Event]] whose [[eventAttendanceMode]] is [[OfflineEventAttendanceMode]] (or the offline aspects, in the case of a [[MixedEventAttendanceMode]]).

properties.name: maximumVirtualAttendeeCapacity
properties.definition: The maximum virtual attendee capacity of an [[Event]] whose [[eventAttendanceMode]] is [[OnlineEventAttendanceMode]] (or the online aspects, in the case of a [[MixedEventAttendanceMode]]).

properties.name: offers
properties.definition: An offer to provide this item—for example, an offer to sell a product, rent the DVD of a movie, perform a service, or give away tickets to an event. Use [[businessFunction]] to indicate the kind of transaction offered, i.e. sell, lease, etc. This property can also be used to describe a [[Demand]]. While this property is listed as expected on a number of common types, it can be used in others. In that case, using a second type, such as Product or a subtype of Product, can clarify the nature of the offer.

properties.name: performer
properties.definition: A performer at the event—for example, a presenter, musician, musical group or actor.

properties.name: performers
properties.definition: The main performer or performers of the event—for example, a presenter, musician, or actor.

properties.name: previousStartDate
properties.definition: Used in conjunction with eventStatus for rescheduled or cancelled events. This property contains the previously scheduled start date. For rescheduled events, the startDate property should be used for the newly scheduled start date. In the (rare) case of an event that has been postponed and rescheduled multiple times, this field may be repeated.

properties.name: recordedIn
properties.definition: The CreativeWork that captured all or part of this Event.

properties.name: remainingAttendeeCapacity
properties.definition: The number of attendee places for an event that remain unallocated.

properties.name: sponsor
properties.definition: A person or organization that supports a thing through a pledge, promise, or financial contribution. E.g. a sponsor of a Medical Study or a corporate sponsor of an event.

properties.name: startDate
properties.definition: The start date and time of the item (in [ISO 8601 date format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)).

properties.name: subEvent
properties.definition: An Event that is part of this event. For example, a conference event includes many presentations, each of which is a subEvent of the conference.

properties.name: subEvents
properties.definition: Events that are a part of this event. For example, a conference event includes many presentations, each subEvents of the conference.

properties.name: superEvent
properties.definition: An event that this event is a part of. For example, a collection of individual music performances might each have a music festival as their superEvent.

properties.name: translator
properties.definition: Organization or person who adapts a creative work to different languages, regional differences and technical requirements of a target market, or that translates during some event.

properties.name: typicalAgeRange
properties.definition: The typical expected age range, e.g. '7-9', '11-'.

properties.name: workFeatured
properties.definition:
A work featured in some event, e.g. exhibited in an ExhibitionEvent.
       Specific subproperties are available for workPerformed (e.g. a play), or a workPresented (a Movie at a ScreeningEvent).

properties.name: workPerformed
properties.definition: A work performed in some event, for example a play performed in a TheaterEvent.

classes.name: Event
classes.definition: An event happening at a certain time and location.

classes.name: EventDetection
classes.definition: Detected spike events from voltage trace(s).

properties.name: EventDetection__detection_method
properties.definition: Description of how events were detected, such as voltage threshold, or dV/dT threshold, as well as relevant values.

properties.name: EventDetection__source_electricalseries
properties.definition: Link to the ElectricalSeries that this data was calculated from. Metadata about electrodes and their position can be read from that ElectricalSeries so it is not necessary to include that information here.

properties.name: EventDetection__source_idx
properties.definition: Indices (zero-based) into the linked source ElectricalSeries::data array corresponding to the time of each event, or the time and channel of each event. ''description'' should define what is meant by time of event (e.g., .25 ms before action potential peak, zero-crossing time, etc.). The index points to each event from the raw data.

properties.name: EventDetection__times
properties.definition: DEPRECATED. Timestamps of events, in seconds.

classes.name: EventWaveform
classes.definition: DEPRECATED. Represents either the waveforms of detected events, as extracted from a raw data trace in /acquisition, or the event waveforms that were stored during experiment acquisition.

classes.name: Events
classes.definition: Event timing information from a behavioral task.

classes.name: Excelitas_Technologies
classes.definition: Model Excelitas Technologies

classes.name: Expadc
classes.definition:
Exponential ADC / Attenuation Coefficient / Attenuation Factor map,
as the ratio of trace-weighted to non-diffusion-weighted image intensity

classes.name: ExternalDataStream
classes.definition: A simplified data stream for acquisitions where instrument metadata is unavailable.

properties.name: ExternalDataStream__modalities
properties.definition: Modalities that are acquired in this stream

classes.name: EyeTracking
classes.definition: Eye-tracking data, representing direction of gaze.

classes.name: Fa
classes.definition:
Scanner-generated derivative parametric map
reflecting the anisotropy of the diffusion tensor

classes.name: FeatureExtraction
classes.definition: Features, such as PC1 and PC2, that are extracted from signals stored in a SpikeEventSeries or other source.

properties.name: FeatureExtraction__description
properties.definition: Description of features (e.g., ''PC1'') for each of the extracted features.

properties.name: FeatureExtraction__electrodes
properties.definition: DynamicTableRegion pointer to the electrodes that this time series was generated from.

properties.name: FeatureExtraction__features
properties.definition: Multi-dimensional array of features extracted from each event.

properties.name: FeatureExtraction__times
properties.definition: Times of events that features correspond to (can be a link).

classes.name: Fib
classes.definition: Model fib

classes.name: FiberAssembly
classes.definition: Module for inserted fiber photometry recording

properties.name: FiberAssembly__fibers
properties.definition: Probes that are held by this module

classes.name: FiberAssemblyConfig
classes.definition: Inserted fiber photometry probe recorded in a stream

properties.name: FiberAssemblyConfig__device_name
properties.definition: Must match a device defined in the instrument.json

classes.name: FiberPatchCord
classes.definition: Description of a patch cord

classes.name: FiberProbe
classes.definition: Description of a fiber optic probe

classes.name: Fieldmap
classes.definition:
Some MR schemes such as spiral-echo imaging (SEI) sequences are able to
directly provide maps of the *B<sub>0</sub>* field inhomogeneity.

classes.name: Filter
classes.definition: Filter used in a light path

properties.name: Filter__center_wavelength
properties.definition: Single wavelength or list of wavelengths for MULTIBAND or MULTI_NOTCH filters

classes.name: FilteredEphys
classes.definition: Electrophysiology data from one or more channels that has been subjected to filtering. Examples of filtered data include Theta and Gamma (LFP has its own interface). FilteredEphys modules publish an ElectricalSeries for each filtered channel or set of channels. The name of each ElectricalSeries is arbitrary but should be informative. The source of the filtered data, whether this is from analysis of another time series or as acquired by hardware, should be noted in the TimeSeries::description field of each ElectricalSeries. There is no assumed 1:1 correspondence between filtered ephys signals and electrodes, as a single signal can apply to many nearby electrodes, and one electrode may have different filtered (e.g., theta and/or gamma) signals represented. Filter properties should be noted in the ElectricalSeries 'filtering' attribute.

classes.name: Flair
classes.definition:
In arbitrary units (arbitrary).
Structural images with predominant T2 contribution (also known as T2-FLAIR),
in which signal from fluids (for example, CSF) is nulled out by adjusting
inversion time, coupled with notably long repetition and echo times.

classes.name: Flash
classes.definition:
FLASH (Fast-Low-Angle-Shot) is a vendor-specific implementation for spoiled
gradient echo acquisition.
It is commonly used for rapid anatomical imaging and also for many different
qMRI applications.
When used for a single file, it does not convey any information about the
image contrast.
When used in a file collection, it may result in conflicts across filenames of
different applications.
**Change:** Removed from suffixes.

classes.name: ImageMaskSeries
classes.definition: DEPRECATED. An alpha mask that is applied to a presented visual stimulus. The 'data' array contains an array of mask values that are applied to the displayed image. Mask values are stored as RGBA. Mask can vary with time. The timestamps array indicates the starting time of a mask, and that mask pattern continues until it is explicitly changed.

properties.name: ImageMaskSeries__masked_imageseries
properties.definition: Link to ImageSeries object that this image mask is applied to.

properties.name: ImageSPIM__file_name

properties.name: ImageSPIM__image_to_acquisition_transform

properties.name: ImageSPIM__imaging_angle
properties.definition: Angle of the detector relative to the image plane relative to perpendicular

classes.name: ImageSegmentation
classes.definition: Stores pixels in an image that represent different regions of interest (ROIs) or masks. All segmentation for a given imaging plane is stored together, with storage for multiple imaging planes (masks) supported. Each ROI is stored in its own subgroup, with the ROI group containing both a 2-D mask and a list of pixels that make up this mask. Segments can also be used for masking neuropil. If segmentation is allowed to change with time, a new imaging plane (or module) is required and ROI names should remain consistent between them.

classes.name: ImageSeries
classes.definition: General image data that is common between acquisition and stimulus time series. Sometimes the image data is stored in the file in a raw format while other times it will be stored as a series of external image files in the host file system. The data field will either be binary data, if the data is stored in the NWB file, or empty, if the data is stored in an external image stack. [frame][x][y] or [frame][x][y][z].

properties.name: ImageSeries__data
properties.definition: Binary data representing images across frames. If data are stored in an external file, this should be an empty 3-D array.

properties.name: ImageSeries__device
properties.definition: Link to the Device object that was used to capture these images.

properties.name: ImageSeries__dimension
properties.definition: Number of pixels on x, y, (and z) axes.

properties.name: ImageSeries__external_file
properties.definition: Paths to one or more external file(s). The field is only present if format='external'. This is only relevant if the image series is stored in the file system as one or more image file(s). This field should NOT be used if the image is stored in another NWB file and that file is linked to this file.

properties.name: ImageSeries__format
properties.definition: Format of image. If this is 'external', then the attribute 'external_file' contains the path information to the image files. If this is 'raw', then the raw (single-channel) binary data is stored in the 'data' dataset. If this attribute is not present, then the default format='raw' case is assumed.

properties.name: ImageSeries__num_samples
properties.definition: Total number of frames across all external files. This is required when format='external' and timing is described using starting_time and rate, since data is empty and its first dimension cannot be used to determine the number of frames. When timestamps is provided, len(timestamps) already serves this purpose.

classes.name: Images
classes.definition: A collection of images with an optional way to specify the order of the images using the "order_of_images" dataset. An order must be specified if the images are referenced by index, e.g., from an IndexSeries.

properties.name: Images__description
properties.definition: Description of this collection of images.

properties.name: Images__order_of_images
properties.definition: Ordered dataset of references to BaseImage objects stored in the parent group. Each object in the Images group should be stored once and only once, so the dataset should have the same length as the number of images.

classes.name: ImagingConfig
classes.definition: Configuration of an imaging instrument

properties.name: ImagingConfig__coordinate_system

properties.name: ImagingConfig__device_name
properties.definition: Must match a device defined in the instrument.json

classes.name: ImagingPlane
classes.definition: An imaging plane and its metadata.

properties.name: ImagingPlane__device
properties.definition: Link to the Device object (e.g., microscope) used to acquire images from this imaging plane.

properties.name: ImagingPlane__grid_spacing
properties.definition: Space between pixels in (x, y) or voxels in (x, y, z) directions, in the specified unit. Assumes imaging plane is a regular grid. See also reference_frame to interpret the grid.

properties.name: ImagingPlane__imaging_rate
properties.definition: Rate that images are acquired, in Hz. If the corresponding TimeSeries is present, the rate should be stored there instead.

properties.name: ImagingPlane__location
properties.definition: Location of the imaging plane. Specify the area, layer, comments on estimation of area/layer, stereotaxic coordinates if in vivo, etc. Use standard atlas names for anatomical regions when possible.

properties.name: ImagingPlane__manifold
properties.definition: DEPRECATED. Physical position of each pixel. 'xyz' represents the position of the pixel relative to the defined coordinate space. Deprecated in favor of origin_coords and grid_spacing.

properties.name: ImagingPlane__origin_coords
properties.definition: Physical location of the first element of the imaging plane (0, 0) for 2-D data or (0, 0, 0) for 3-D data. See also reference_frame for what the physical location is relative to (e.g., bregma).

properties.name: ImagingPlane__reference_frame
properties.definition: Describes reference frame of origin_coords and grid_spacing. For example, this can be a text description of the anatomical location and orientation of the grid defined by origin_coords and grid_spacing or the vectors needed to transform or rotate the grid to a common anatomical axis (e.g., AP/DV/ML). This field is necessary to interpret origin_coords and grid_spacing. If origin_coords and grid_spacing are not present, then this field is not required. For example, if the microscope takes 10 x 10 x 2 images, where the first value of the data matrix (index (0, 0, 0)) corresponds to (-1.2, -0.6, -2) mm relative to bregma, the spacing between pixels is 0.2 mm in x, 0.2 mm in y and 0.5 mm in z, and larger numbers in x mean more anterior, larger numbers in y mean more rightward, and larger numbers in z mean more ventral, then enter the following -- origin_coords = (-1.2, -0.6, -2) grid_spacing = (0.2, 0.2, 0.5) reference_frame = "Origin coordinates are relative to bregma. First dimension corresponds to anterior-posterior axis (larger index = more anterior). Second dimension corresponds to medial-lateral axis (larger index = more rightward). Third dimension corresponds to dorsal-ventral axis (larger index = more ventral)."

classes.name: ImagingRetinotopy
classes.definition: DEPRECATED. Intrinsic signal optical imaging or widefield imaging for measuring retinotopy. Stores orthogonal maps (e.g., altitude/azimuth; radius/theta) of responses to specific stimuli and a combined polarity map from which to identify visual areas. This group does not store the raw responses imaged during retinotopic mapping or the stimuli presented, but rather the resulting phase and power maps after applying a Fourier transform on the averaged responses. Note: for data consistency, all images and arrays are stored in the format [row][column] and [row, col], which equates to [y][x]. Field of view and dimension arrays may appear backward (i.e., y before x).

properties.name: ImagingRetinotopy__axis_1_phase_map
properties.definition: Phase response to stimulus on the first measured axis.

properties.name: ImagingRetinotopy__axis_1_power_map
properties.definition: Power response on the first measured axis. Response is scaled so 0.0 is no power in the response and 1.0 is maximum relative power.

properties.name: ImagingRetinotopy__axis_2_phase_map
properties.definition: Phase response to stimulus on the second measured axis.

properties.name: ImagingRetinotopy__axis_2_power_map
properties.definition: Power response on the second measured axis. Response is scaled so 0.0 is no power in the response and 1.0 is maximum relative power.

properties.name: ImagingRetinotopy__axis_descriptions
properties.definition: Two-element array describing the contents of the two response axis fields. Description should be something like ['altitude', 'azimuth'] or ['radius', 'theta'].

properties.name: ImagingRetinotopy__focal_depth_image
properties.definition: Gray-scale image taken with the same settings/parameters (e.g., focal depth, wavelength) as data collection. Array format: [rows][columns].

properties.name: ImagingRetinotopy__sign_map
properties.definition: Sine of the angle between the direction of the gradient in axis_1 and axis_2.

properties.name: ImagingRetinotopy__vasculature_image
properties.definition: Gray-scale anatomical image of cortical surface. Array structure: [rows][columns]

classes.name: IndexSeries
classes.definition: Stores indices that reference images defined in other containers. The primary purpose of the IndexSeries is to allow images stored in an Images container to be referenced in a specific sequence through the 'indexed_images' link. This approach avoids duplicating image data when the same image needs to be presented multiple times or when images need to be shown in a different order than they are stored. Since images in an Images container do not have an inherent order, the Images container needs to include an 'order_of_images' dataset (of type ImageReferences) whenever it is referenced by an IndexSeries. This dataset establishes the ordered sequence that the indices in IndexSeries refer to. The 'data' field stores the index into this ordered sequence, and the 'timestamps' array indicates the precise presentation time of each indexed image during an experiment. This can be used for displaying individual images or creating movie segments by referencing a sequence of images with the appropriate timestamps. While IndexSeries can also reference frames from an ImageSeries through the 'indexed_timeseries' link, this usage is discouraged and will be deprecated in favor of using Images containers with 'order_of_images'.

properties.name: IndexSeries__data
properties.definition: Index of the image (using zero-indexing) in the linked Images object.

properties.name: IndexSeries__indexed_images
properties.definition: Link to Images object containing an ordered set of images that are indexed. The Images object must contain an 'order_of_images' dataset specifying the order of the images in the Images type.

properties.name: IndexSeries__indexed_timeseries
properties.definition: Link to ImageSeries object containing images that are indexed. Use of this link is discouraged and will be deprecated. Link to an Images type instead.

A binary mask that functions as a discrete "label" for a single structure.

This suffix may only be used in derivative datasets.

classes.definition: Identifier for measurement technique used

properties.name: MeasurementTechniqueType__identifier

classes.name: MedicalCondition
classes.definition: Any condition of the human body that affects the normal functioning of a person, whether physically or mentally. Includes diseases, injuries, disabilities, disorders, syndromes, etc.

properties.name: associatedAnatomy
properties.definition: The anatomy of the underlying organ system or structures associated with this entity.

properties.name: cause
properties.definition: The cause of a medical condition.

properties.name: differentialDiagnosis
properties.definition: One of a set of differential diagnoses for the condition. Specifically, a closely-related or competing diagnosis typically considered later in the cognitive process whereby this medical condition is distinguished from others most likely responsible for a similar collection of signs and symptoms to reach the most parsimonious diagnosis or diagnoses in a patient.

properties.name: drug
properties.definition: Specifying a drug or medicine used in a medication procedure.

properties.name: epidemiology
properties.definition: The characteristics of associated patients, such as age, gender, race etc.

properties.name: expectedPrognosis
properties.definition: The likely outcome in either the short term or long term of the medical condition.

properties.name: naturalProgression
properties.definition: The expected progression of the condition if it is not treated and allowed to progress naturally.

properties.name: pathophysiology
properties.definition: Changes in the normal mechanical, physical, and biochemical functions that are associated with this activity or condition.

properties.name: possibleComplication
properties.definition: A possible unexpected and unfavorable evolution of a medical condition. Complications may include worsening of the signs or symptoms of the disease, extension of the condition to other organ systems, etc.

properties.name: possibleTreatment
properties.definition: A possible treatment to address this condition, sign or symptom.

properties.name: primaryPrevention
properties.definition: A preventative therapy used to prevent an initial occurrence of the medical condition, such as vaccination.

properties.name: riskFactor
properties.definition: A modifiable or non-modifiable factor that increases the risk of a patient contracting this condition, e.g. age,  coexisting condition.

properties.name: secondaryPrevention
properties.definition: A preventative therapy used to prevent reoccurrence of the medical condition after an initial episode of the condition.

properties.name: signOrSymptom
properties.definition: A sign or symptom of this condition. Signs are objective or physically observable manifestations of the medical condition while symptoms are the subjective experience of the medical condition.

properties.name: stage
properties.definition: The stage of the condition, if applicable.

properties.name: typicalTest
properties.definition: A medical test typically performed given this condition.

classes.name: MedicalDevice
classes.definition: Any object used in a medical capacity, such as to diagnose or treat a patient.

properties.name: adverseOutcome
properties.definition: A possible complication and/or side effect of this therapy. If it is known that an adverse outcome is serious (resulting in death, disability, or permanent damage; requiring hospitalization; or otherwise life-threatening or requiring immediate medical attention), tag it as a seriousAdverseOutcome instead.

properties.name: postOp
properties.definition: A description of the postoperative procedures, care, and/or followups for this device.

properties.name: preOp
properties.definition: A description of the workup, testing, and other preparations required before implanting this device.

properties.name: procedure
properties.definition: A description of the procedure involved in setting up, using, and/or installing the device.

properties.name: seriousAdverseOutcome
properties.definition: A possible serious complication and/or serious side effect of this therapy. Serious adverse outcomes include those that are life-threatening; result in death, disability, or permanent damage; require hospitalization or prolong existing hospitalization; cause congenital anomalies or birth defects; or jeopardize the patient and may require medical or surgical intervention to prevent one of the outcomes in this definition.

classes.name: MedicalEntity
classes.definition: The most generic type of entity related to health and the practice of medicine.

properties.name: code
properties.definition: A medical code for the entity, taken from a controlled vocabulary or ontology such as ICD-9, DiseasesDB, MeSH, SNOMED-CT, RxNorm, etc.

properties.name: funding
properties.definition: A [[Grant]] that directly or indirectly provide funding or sponsorship for this item. See also [[ownershipFundingInfo]].

properties.name: guideline
properties.definition: A medical guideline related to this entity.

properties.name: legalStatus
properties.definition: The drug or supplement's legal status, including any controlled substance schedules that apply.

properties.name: medicineSystem
properties.definition: The system of medicine that includes this MedicalEntity, for example 'evidence-based', 'homeopathic', 'chiropractic', etc.

properties.name: recognizingAuthority
properties.definition: If applicable, the organization that officially recognizes this entity as part of its endorsed system of medicine.

properties.name: relevantSpecialty
properties.definition: If applicable, a medical specialty in which this entity is relevant.

properties.name: study
properties.definition: A medical study or trial related to this entity.

classes.name: MedicalObservationalStudy
classes.definition: An observational study is a type of medical study that attempts to infer the possible effect of a treatment through observation of a cohort of subjects over a period of time. In an observational study, the assignment of subjects into treatment groups versus control groups is outside the control of the investigator. This is in contrast with controlled studies, such as the randomized controlled trials represented by MedicalTrial, where each subject is randomly assigned to a treatment group or a control group before the start of the treatment.

properties.name: studyDesign
properties.definition: Specifics about the observational study design (enumerated).

classes.name: MedicalProcedure
classes.definition: A process of care used in either a diagnostic, therapeutic, preventive or palliative capacity that relies on invasive (surgical), non-invasive, or other techniques.

properties.name: bodyLocation
properties.definition: Location in the body of the anatomical structure.

properties.name: followup
properties.definition: Typical or recommended followup care after the procedure is performed.

properties.name: preparation
properties.definition: Typical preparation that a patient must undergo before having the procedure performed.

properties.name: procedureType
properties.definition: The type of procedure, for example Surgical, Noninvasive, or Percutaneous.

classes.name: MedicalSignOrSymptom
classes.definition: Any feature associated or not with a medical condition. In medicine a symptom is generally subjective while a sign is objective.

properties.name: possibleTreatment
properties.definition: A possible treatment to address this condition, sign or symptom.

classes.name: MedicalStudy
classes.definition: A medical study is an umbrella type covering all kinds of research studies relating to human medicine or health, including observational studies and interventional trials and registries, randomized, controlled or not. When the specific type of study is known, use one of the extensions of this type, such as MedicalTrial or MedicalObservationalStudy. Also, note that this type should be used to mark up data that describes the study itself; to tag an article that publishes the results of a study, use MedicalScholarlyArticle. Note: use the code property of MedicalEntity to store study IDs, e.g. clinicaltrials.gov ID.

properties.name: healthCondition
properties.definition: Specifying the health condition(s) of a patient, medical study, or other target audience.

properties.name: sponsor
properties.definition: A person or organization that supports a thing through a pledge, promise, or financial contribution. E.g. a sponsor of a Medical Study or a corporate sponsor of an event.

properties.name: studyLocation
properties.definition: The location in which the study is taking/took place.

properties.name: studySubject
properties.definition: A subject of the study, i.e. one of the medical conditions, therapies, devices, drugs, etc. investigated by the study.

classes.name: MedicalTest
classes.definition: Any medical test, typically performed for diagnostic purposes.

properties.name: affectedBy
properties.definition: Drugs that affect the test's results.

properties.name: normalRange
properties.definition: Range of acceptable values for a typical patient, when applicable.

properties.name: signDetected
properties.definition: A sign detected by the test.

properties.name: usedToDiagnose
properties.definition: A condition the test is used to diagnose.

properties.name: usesDevice
properties.definition: Device used to perform the test.

classes.name: MedicalTrial
classes.definition: A medical trial is a type of medical study that uses a scientific process to compare the safety and efficacy of medical therapies or medical procedures. In general, medical trials are controlled and subjects are allocated at random to the different treatment and/or control groups.

classes.name: Meg
classes.definition: Magnetoencephalography

classes.name: Megre
classes.definition:
Anatomical gradient echo images acquired at different echo times.
Please note that this suffix is not intended for the logical grouping of
images acquired using an Echo Planar Imaging (EPI) readout.

classes.name: Merfish
classes.definition: Model merfish

classes.name: Mese
classes.definition:
The MESE method involves multiple spin echo images acquired at different echo
times and is primarily used for T2 mapping.
Please note that this suffix is not intended for the logical grouping of
images acquired using an Echo Planar Imaging (EPI) readout.

classes.name: Metadata

The records in the Data Asset Collection needs to contain certain fields
to easily query and index the data.

properties.name: Metadata__location

properties.name: Metadata__model

properties.name: Metadata__other_identifiers
properties.definition: Links to the data asset on secondary platforms.

properties.name: Metadata__procedures
properties.definition: All procedures performed on a subject.

properties.name: Metadata__quality_control
properties.definition: Description of quality metrics for a data asset

properties.name: ModelTraining__output_path

properties.name: ModelTraining__test_evaluation_method

classes.name: MolecularEntity
classes.definition: Any constitutionally or isotopically distinct atom, molecule, ion, ion pair, radical, radical ion, complex, conformer etc., identifiable as a separately distinguishable entity.

properties.name: chemicalRole
properties.definition: A role played by the BioChemEntity within a chemical context.

properties.name: inChI
properties.definition: Non-proprietary identifier for molecular entity that can be used in printed and electronic data sources thus enabling easier linking of diverse data compilations.

properties.name: inChIKey
properties.definition: InChIKey is a hashed version of the full InChI (using the SHA-256 algorithm).

properties.name: iupacName
properties.definition: Systematic method of naming chemical compounds as recommended by the International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC).

properties.name: molecularFormula
properties.definition: The empirical formula is the simplest whole number ratio of all the atoms in a molecule.

properties.name: molecularWeight
properties.definition: This is the molecular weight of the entity being described, not of the parent. Units should be included in the form '<Number> <unit>', for example '12 amu' or as '<QuantitativeValue>.

properties.name: monoisotopicMolecularWeight
properties.definition: The monoisotopic mass is the sum of the masses of the atoms in a molecule using the unbound, ground-state, rest mass of the principal (most abundant) isotope for each element instead of the isotopic average mass. Please include the units in the form '<Number> <unit>', for example '770.230488 g/mol' or as '<QuantitativeValue>.

properties.name: potentialUse
properties.definition: Intended use of the BioChemEntity by humans.

properties.name: smiles
properties.definition: A specification in form of a line notation for describing the structure of chemical species using short ASCII strings.  Double bond stereochemistry \ indicators may need to be escaped in the string in formats where the backslash is an escape character.

properties.name: MouseSubject__restrictions

classes.name: Mp2rage
classes.definition:
The MP2RAGE method is a special protocol that collects several images at
different flip angles and inversion times to create a parametric T1map by
combining the magnitude and phase images
([Marques et al. 2010](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuroimage.2009.10.002)).

classes.name: Mpe
classes.definition: Multi-photon excitation microscopy imaging data

classes.name: Mpi
classes.definition: Model MPI

classes.name: Mpm
classes.definition:
The MPM approaches (a.k.a hMRI) involves the acquisition of highly-similar
anatomical images that differ in terms of application of a magnetization
transfer RF pulse (MTon or MToff), flip angle and (optionally) echo time and
magnitue/phase parts
([Weiskopf et al. 2013](https://doi.org/10.3389/fnins.2013.00095)).
See [here](https://owncloud.gwdg.de/index.php/s/iv2TOQwGy4FGDDZ) for
suggested MPM acquisition protocols.

classes.name: Mri
classes.definition: Model MRI

classes.name: Mrs
classes.definition: Magnetic resonance spectroscopy data

classes.name: Mrsi
classes.definition:
MRS acquisitions where additional imaging gradients are used
to detect the MR signal from 1, 2, or 3 spatial dimensions.

classes.name: Mrsref
classes.definition:
An MRS acquisition collected to serve as a concentration reference for absolute quantification
or as a calibration reference for preprocessing (for example, eddy-current correction).

classes.name: Mtr
classes.definition:
This method is to calculate a semi-quantitative magnetization transfer ratio
map.

classes.name: Mtrmap
classes.definition:
In arbitrary units (arbitrary).
MTR maps are REQUIRED to use this suffix regardless of the method used to
generate them.
MTRmap intensity values are RECOMMENDED to be represented in percentage in
the range of 0-100%.

classes.name: Mts
classes.definition:
This method is to calculate a semi-quantitative magnetization transfer
saturation index map.
The MTS method involves three sets of anatomical images that differ in terms
of application of a magnetization transfer RF pulse (MTon or MToff) and flip
angle ([Helms et al. 2008](https://doi.org/10.1002/mrm.21732)).

classes.name: Mtsat
classes.definition:
In arbitrary units (arbitrary).
MTsat maps are REQUIRED to use this suffix regardless of the method used to
generate them.

classes.name: Mtvmap
classes.definition:
In arbitrary units (arbitrary).
MTV maps are REQUIRED to use this suffix regardless of the method used to
generate them.

classes.name: Multipwmgenerator
classes.definition: Model MultiPwmGenerator

classes.name: Mus_Musculus
classes.definition: Model Mus musculus

classes.name: Mwfmap
classes.definition:
In arbitrary units (arbitrary).
MWF maps are REQUIRED to use this suffix regardless of the method used to
generate them.
MWF intensity values are RECOMMENDED to be represented in percentage in the
range of 0-100%.

classes.name: NWBContainer
classes.definition: An abstract data type for a generic container storing collections of data and metadata. Base type for all data and metadata containers.

classes.name: NWBDataInterface
classes.definition: An abstract data type for a generic container storing collections of data, as opposed to metadata.

classes.name: NWBFile
classes.definition: An NWB file storing cellular-based neurophysiology data from a single experimental session.

properties.name: NWBFile__file_create_date
properties.definition: A record of the date the file was created and of subsequent modifications. The date is stored in UTC with local timezone offset as ISO 8601 extended formatted strings: 2018-09-28T14:43:54.123+02:00. Dates stored in UTC end in "Z" with no timezone offset. Date accuracy is up to milliseconds. The file can be created after the experiment was run, so this may differ from the experiment start time. Each modification to the nwb file adds a new entry to the array.

properties.name: NWBFile__identifier
properties.definition: A unique text identifier for the file. For example, concatenated lab name, file creation date/time and experimentalist, or a hash of these and/or other values. The goal is that the string should be unique to all other files.

properties.name: NWBFile__nwb_version
properties.definition: File version string. Use semantic versioning, e.g., 1.2.1. This will be the name of the format with trailing major, minor and patch numbers.

properties.name: NWBFile__session_description
properties.definition: A description of the experimental session and data in the file.

properties.name: NWBFile__session_start_time
properties.definition: Date and time of the experiment/session start. The date is stored in UTC with local timezone offset as ISO 8601 extended formatted string: 2018-09-28T14:43:54.123+02:00. Dates stored in UTC end in "Z" with no timezone offset. Date accuracy is up to milliseconds.

properties.name: NWBFile__timestamps_reference_time
properties.definition: Date and time corresponding to time zero of all timestamps. The date is stored in UTC with local timezone offset as ISO 8601 extended formatted string: 2018-09-28T14:43:54.123+02:00. Dates stored in UTC end in "Z" with no timezone offset. Date accuracy is up to milliseconds. All times stored in the file use this time as reference (i.e., time zero).

classes.name: Norf
classes.definition:
An MR noise scan often acquired alongside another MR scan.

These noise scans are acquired by disabling the radio frequency excitation,
while maintaining all other parameters from the associated scan.
These can be acquired using some other sequence with RF excitation removed
or as part of sequences that turns off RF at the beginning or end of a run.
For example a `bold` scan where a few volumes are acquired with RF off.
There is no consistent DICOM denotation for these scans.

If multiple sequences with different suffixes but otherwise the same entities have `noRF`
sequences, they SHOULD be disambiguated using the modality entity
(for example, `mod-bold_noRF.nii.gz` and `mod-cbv_noRF.nii`).

`noRF` are typically used to estimate properties of the scanner and
sequence that are independent from the object being imaged.

properties.name: OnePhotonSeries__binning
properties.definition: Amount of pixels combined into 'bins'; could be 1, 2, 4, 8, etc.

properties.name: OnePhotonSeries__data
properties.definition:
Time-series of microscopy data indexed as data[time, width, height].

Indexing convention:
  - width: first spatial axis (horizontal direction, columns)
  - height: second spatial axis (vertical direction, rows)

For raster-scanning systems (two-photon, confocal, one-photon laser scanning):
  - width: fast scan direction (horizontal)
  - height: slow scan direction (vertical)

Coordinate system diagram::

  height  ^
  (slow)  |
          |    +-----------------+
          |    |                 |
          |    |   Image plane   |
          |    |                 |
          |    +-----------------+
          |
          0 ----------------------> width (fast)


For non-raster systems (wide-field, light-sheet, random-access), width and height
are arbitrary coordinates of the image plane.

Note: This indexing convention conflicts with standard matrix[row, column] notation
for array access where the first index moves vertically through rows and the second moves horizontally
through columns. In the schema, data[time, width, height] means that the first spatial
index moves horizontally (width) through columns and the second spatial index moves
vertically (height) through rows. In summary, incrementing the first spatial index
moves you horizontally across the image, while in matrix notation it would move you
vertically across the rows of the image.

properties.name: OnePhotonSeries__exposure_time
properties.definition: Exposure time of the sample; often the inverse of the frequency.

properties.name: OnePhotonSeries__imaging_plane
properties.definition: Link to ImagingPlane object from which this TimeSeries data was generated.

properties.name: OnePhotonSeries__intensity
properties.definition: Intensity of the excitation in mW/mm^2, if known.

properties.name: OnePhotonSeries__power
properties.definition: Power of the excitation in mW, if known.

properties.name: OnePhotonSeries__scan_line_rate
properties.definition: Lines imaged per second. This is also stored in /general/optophysiology but is kept here as it is useful information for analysis, and is therefore stored with the actual data.

classes.name: OpticalSeries
classes.definition: Image data that is presented or recorded. A stimulus template movie will be stored only as an image. When the image is presented as stimulus, additional data is required, such as field of view (e.g., how much of the visual field the image covers, or how large the imaged area of the target is). If the OpticalSeries represents acquired imaging data, orientation is also important.

properties.name: OpticalSeries__data
properties.definition: Images presented to subject, either grayscale or RGB

properties.name: OpticalSeries__field_of_view
properties.definition: Width, height and depth of image, or imaged area, in meters.

properties.name: OpticalSeries__orientation
properties.definition: Description of image relative to some reference frame (e.g., which way is up). Must also specify frame of reference.

properties.name: OptogeneticSeries__site
properties.definition: Link to OptogeneticStimulusSite object that describes the site to which this stimulus was applied.

classes.name: OptogeneticStimulusSite

properties.name: OptogeneticStimulusSite__location

classes.name: Organization

properties.name: acceptedPaymentMethod
properties.definition: The payment method(s) that are accepted in general by an organization, or for some specific demand or offer.

properties.name: actionableFeedbackPolicy
properties.definition: For a [[NewsMediaOrganization]] or other news-related [[Organization]], a statement about public engagement activities (for news media, the newsroom’s), including involving the public - digitally or otherwise -- in coverage decisions, reporting and activities after publication.

properties.name: agentInteractionStatistic
properties.definition: The number of completed interactions for this entity, in a particular role (the 'agent'), in a particular action (indicated in the statistic), and in a particular context (i.e. interactionService).

properties.name: aggregateRating
properties.definition: The overall rating, based on a collection of reviews or ratings, of the item.

properties.name: areaServed
properties.definition: The geographic area where a service or offered item is provided.

properties.name: award
properties.definition: An award won by or for this item.

properties.name: awards
properties.definition: Awards won by or for this item.

properties.name: brand
properties.definition: The brand(s) associated with a product or service, or the brand(s) maintained by an organization or business person.

properties.name: companyRegistration
properties.definition: The official registration information of a business including the organization that issued it such as Company House or Chamber of Commerce in form of a Certification.

properties.name: contactPoint
properties.definition: A contact point for a person or organization.

properties.name: contactPoints
properties.definition: A contact point for a person or organization.

properties.name: correctionsPolicy
properties.definition: For an [[Organization]] (e.g. [[NewsMediaOrganization]]), a statement describing (in news media, the newsroom’s) disclosure and correction policy for errors.

properties.name: department
properties.definition: A relationship between an organization and a department of that organization, also described as an organization (allowing different urls, logos, opening hours). For example: a store with a pharmacy, or a bakery with a cafe.

properties.name: dissolutionDate
properties.definition: The date that this organization was dissolved.

properties.name: diversityPolicy
properties.definition: Statement on diversity policy by an [[Organization]] e.g. a [[NewsMediaOrganization]]. For a [[NewsMediaOrganization]], a statement describing the newsroom’s diversity policy on both staffing and sources, typically providing staffing data.

properties.name: diversityStaffingReport
properties.definition: For an [[Organization]] (often but not necessarily a [[NewsMediaOrganization]]), a report on staffing diversity issues. In a news context this might be for example ASNE or RTDNA (US) reports, or self-reported.

properties.name: duns
properties.definition: The Dun & Bradstreet DUNS number for identifying an organization or business person.

properties.name: ethicsPolicy
properties.definition: Statement about ethics policy, e.g. of a [[NewsMediaOrganization]] regarding journalistic and publishing practices, or of a [[Restaurant]], a page describing food source policies. In the case of a [[NewsMediaOrganization]], an ethicsPolicy is typically a statement describing the personal, organizational, and corporate standards of behavior expected by the organization.

properties.name: event
properties.definition: Upcoming or past event associated with this place, organization, or action.

properties.name: events
properties.definition: Upcoming or past events associated with this place or organization.

properties.name: founder
properties.definition: A person or organization who founded this organization.

properties.name: founders
properties.definition: A person who founded this organization.

properties.name: foundingDate
properties.definition: The date that this organization was founded.

properties.name: foundingLocation
properties.definition: The place where the Organization was founded.

properties.name: funder
properties.definition: A person or organization that supports (sponsors) something through some kind of financial contribution.

properties.name: funding
properties.definition: A [[Grant]] that directly or indirectly provide funding or sponsorship for this item. See also [[ownershipFundingInfo]].

properties.name: globalLocationNumber
properties.definition: The [Global Location Number](http://www.gs1.org/gln) (GLN, sometimes also referred to as International Location Number or ILN) of the respective organization, person, or place. The GLN is a 13-digit number used to identify parties and physical locations.

properties.name: hasCertification
properties.definition: Certification information about a product, organization, service, place, or person.

properties.name: hasCredential
properties.definition: A credential awarded to the Person or Organization.

properties.name: hasGS1DigitalLink
properties.definition:
The GS1 digital link associated with the object. This URL should conform to the particular requirements of digital links. The link should only contain the Application Identifiers (AIs) that are relevant for the entity being annotated, for instance a [[Product]] or an [[Organization]], and for the correct granularity. In particular, for products:
  • A Digital Link that contains a serial number (AI 21) should only be present on instances of [[IndividualProduct]]
  • A Digital Link that contains a lot number (AI 10) should be annotated as [[SomeProducts]] if only products from that lot are sold, or [[IndividualProduct]] if there is only a specific product.
  • A Digital Link that contains a global model number (AI 8013) should be attached to a [[Product]] or a [[ProductModel]].
Other item types should be adapted similarly.

properties.name: hasMemberProgram
properties.definition: MemberProgram offered by an Organization, for example an eCommerce merchant or an airline.

properties.name: hasMerchantReturnPolicy
properties.definition: Specifies a MerchantReturnPolicy that may be applicable.

properties.name: hasOfferCatalog
properties.definition: Indicates an OfferCatalog listing for this Organization, Person, or Service.

properties.name: hasPOS
properties.definition: Points-of-Sales operated by the organization or person.

properties.name: hasShippingService
properties.definition: Specification of a shipping service offered by the organization.

properties.name: interactionStatistic
properties.definition: The number of interactions for the CreativeWork using the WebSite or SoftwareApplication. The most specific child type of InteractionCounter should be used.

properties.name: isicV4
properties.definition: The International Standard of Industrial Classification of All Economic Activities (ISIC), Revision 4 code for a particular organization, business person, or place.

properties.name: iso6523Code
properties.definition: An organization identifier as defined in [ISO 6523(-1)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO/IEC_6523). The identifier should be in the `XXXX:YYYYYY:ZZZ` or `XXXX:YYYYYY`format. Where `XXXX` is a 4 digit _ICD_ (International Code Designator), `YYYYYY` is an _OID_ (Organization Identifier) with all formatting characters (dots, dashes, spaces) removed with a maximal length of 35 characters, and `ZZZ` is an optional OPI (Organization Part Identifier) with a maximum length of 35 characters. The various components (ICD, OID, OPI) are joined with a colon character (ASCII `0x3a`). Note that many existing organization identifiers defined as attributes like [leiCode](https://schema.org/leiCode) (`0199`), [duns](https://schema.org/duns) (`0060`) or [GLN](https://schema.org/globalLocationNumber) (`0088`) can be expressed using ISO-6523. If possible, ISO-6523 codes should be preferred to populating [vatID](https://schema.org/vatID) or [taxID](https://schema.org/taxID), as ISO identifiers are less ambiguous.

properties.name: keywords
properties.definition: Keywords or tags used to describe some item. Multiple textual entries in a keywords list are typically delimited by commas, or by repeating the property.

properties.name: knowsAbout
properties.definition: Of a [[Person]], and less typically of an [[Organization]], to indicate a topic that is known about - suggesting possible expertise but not implying it. We do not distinguish skill levels here, or relate this to educational content, events, objectives or [[JobPosting]] descriptions.

properties.name: knowsLanguage
properties.definition: Of a [[Person]], and less typically of an [[Organization]], to indicate a known language. We do not distinguish skill levels or reading/writing/speaking/signing here. Use language codes from the [IETF BCP 47 standard](http://tools.ietf.org/html/bcp47).

properties.name: legalAddress
properties.definition: The legal address of an organization which acts as the officially registered address used for legal and tax purposes. The legal address can be different from the place of operations of a business and other addresses can be part of an organization.

properties.name: legalName
properties.definition: The official name of the organization, e.g. the registered company name.

properties.name: legalRepresentative
properties.definition: One or multiple persons who represent this organization legally such as CEO or sole administrator.

properties.name: leiCode
properties.definition: An organization identifier that uniquely identifies a legal entity as defined in ISO 17442.

properties.name: location
properties.definition: The location of, for example, where an event is happening, where an organization is located, or where an action takes place.

properties.name: makesOffer
properties.definition: A pointer to products or services offered by the organization or person.

properties.name: member
properties.definition: A member of an Organization or a ProgramMembership. Organizations can be members of organizations; ProgramMembership is typically for individuals.

properties.name: memberOf
properties.definition: An Organization (or ProgramMembership) to which this Person or Organization belongs.

properties.name: naics
properties.definition: The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code for a particular organization or business person.

properties.name: nonprofitStatus
properties.definition: nonprofitStatus indicates the legal status of a non-profit organization in its primary place of business.

properties.name: numberOfEmployees
properties.definition: The number of employees in an organization, e.g. business.

properties.name: ownershipFundingInfo
properties.definition: For an [[Organization]] (often but not necessarily a [[NewsMediaOrganization]]), a description of organizational ownership structure; funding and grants. In a news/media setting, this is with particular reference to editorial independence.   Note that the [[funder]] is also available and can be used to make basic funder information machine-readable.

properties.name: owns
properties.definition: Things owned by the organization or person.

properties.name: parentOrganization
properties.definition: The larger organization that this organization is a [[subOrganization]] of, if any.

properties.name: publishingPrinciples
properties.definition:
The publishingPrinciples property indicates (typically via [[URL]]) a document describing the editorial principles of an [[Organization]] (or individual, e.g. a [[Person]] writing a blog) that relate to their activities as a publisher, e.g. ethics or diversity policies. When applied to a [[CreativeWork]] (e.g. [[NewsArticle]]) the principles are those of the party primarily responsible for the creation of the [[CreativeWork]].

While such policies are most typically expressed in natural language, sometimes related information (e.g. indicating a [[funder]]) can be expressed using schema.org terminology.

properties.name: seeks
properties.definition: A pointer to products or services sought by the organization or person (demand).

properties.name: serviceArea
properties.definition: The geographic area where the service is provided.

properties.name: skills
properties.definition: A statement of knowledge, skill, ability, task or any other assertion expressing a competency that is either claimed by a person, an organization or desired or required to fulfill a role or to work in an occupation.

properties.name: slogan
properties.definition: A slogan or motto associated with the item.

properties.name: sponsor
properties.definition: A person or organization that supports a thing through a pledge, promise, or financial contribution. E.g. a sponsor of a Medical Study or a corporate sponsor of an event.

properties.name: subOrganization
properties.definition: A relationship between two organizations where the first includes the second, e.g., as a subsidiary. See also: the more specific 'department' property.

properties.name: taxID
properties.definition: The Tax / Fiscal ID of the organization or person, e.g. the TIN in the US or the CIF/NIF in Spain.

properties.name: unnamedSourcesPolicy
properties.definition: For an [[Organization]] (typically a [[NewsMediaOrganization]]), a statement about policy on use of unnamed sources and the decision process required.

properties.name: vatID
properties.definition: The value-added Tax ID of the organization or person with national prefix (for example IT123456789). Can also be described as [[iso6523Code]] with proper prefix.

classes.name: Organization
classes.definition: An organization such as a school, NGO, corporation, club, or funding body.

properties.name: Organization__roleName
properties.definition: Role(s) of the contributor. Multiple roles can be selected.
